name: ats3-lsp-client
description: VSCode client for the ATS3 language server (diagnostics, hover, go-to-definition).

ats3.server.backend.markdownDescription: Which language-server backend to launch. `auto` (default) prefers the **Chez** resident server (xats2cz, `chez --script <so>`) when its `.so` is present, otherwise the self-contained Deno binary. `chez` / `deno` force one.

ats3.server.chezPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the compiled Chez resident server (`chez-lsp-resident.so`, built by `server/chez-build-resident.sh`). When empty, the client auto-resolves it from `server-dist/` (packaged) or the repo `server/BUILD/chez/` (dev).

ats3.server.chezBin.markdownDescription: The Chez Scheme executable used to run the resident server `.so` (default `chez`; set an absolute path if `chez` is not on `PATH`).

ats3.server.debounceMs.markdownDescription: Live-on-change debounce in milliseconds (passed to the server as `ATS3_LSP_DEBOUNCE_MS`). `0` (default) leaves the server's own default (150 ms). Lower it (e.g. `50`) for snappier as-you-type diagnostics at the cost of re-checking more aggressively while typing.

ats3.server.staloadCompletion.markdownDescription: Offer completions for the API a file's `#staload`/`#include` closure brings into scope (not just the file's own symbols + the pervasive prelude). On by default; disabling it (sets `ATS3_LSP_STALOAD_COMPLETE=0`) skips indexing the staloaded interfaces — useful only if the first check of a compiler-linking file is too heavy.

ats3.server.denoPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the compiled Deno server binary (a single executable built by `npm run build:deno` via `deno compile`, with `--allow-all` and `--v8-flags=--stack-size=8801` baked in). When empty, the client auto-resolves it from `server-dist/xats-lsp-deno` (packaged) or the repo `server/resident/BUILD/` (dev).

ats3.xatshome.markdownDescription: Path to the ATS3/Xanadu repo root (`XATSHOME`), needed by the checker to locate the prelude. **Required when the extension is installed from a `.vsix`** — an installed extension has no repo next to it, so it cannot guess this and the server will not start until it is set (or the `XATSHOME` env var is exported for the editor). When running the extension from source (F5 in the repo), it defaults to two directories above the extension (the repo root in the in-tree layout).

ats3.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VSCode and the ATS3 language server.

scripts.package: npm run bundle && npm run stage:chez && vsce package --out ats3-lsp-client.vsix
